preop_fasting: Adequate preoperative fasting: e.g. clear fluids up to 2 h prior to surgery, solids up to 6 h prior to surgery
senior_surgeon: Superspecialist (paediatric surgery, gastrointestinal surgery, surgical oncologist)
wound_class: CLASS III (Contaminated wounds - created when an outside object e.g a bullet, knife blade or other pointy object comes in contact with the wound. Or caused by large amounts of spillage from the GI tract into the wound or any highly inflamed or infected tissue around a surgical wound)
clavien_raw: GRADE I - Any deviation from the normal postoperative course without the need for pharmacological treatment or surgical, endoscopic and radiological interventions. Allowed therapeutic regimens are drugs as antiemetics, antipyretics, analgesics, diuretics and electrolytes and physiotherapy, including wound infections opened at the bedside)
complication_grade: MILD COMPLICATION (results in only temporary harm and does not usually require specific clini-cal treatment)

surg_type_adult: Orthopedic and Trauma
urgency: EMERGENCY (Immediate life, limb or organ-saving intervention - resuscitation simultaneous with intervention. Normally within minutes of decision to operate. A) Life-saving B) Other e.g., limb or organ saving)
preop_fasting: No fasting
wound_class: CLASS I (Clean surgical wounds show no signs of inflammation and do not involve the respiratory, gastrointestinal or genitourinary tracts)
clavien_raw: GRADE II - Requiring pharmacological treatment with drugs other than such allowed for grade I complications. it includes Blood transfusions and total parenteral nutrition.
complication_grade: MODERATE COMPLICATION (a more serious, usually requires clinical treatment but does not usu-ally result in permanent harm or functional limitation)
